explanation.summary: The controlling expression of the 'if' statement is 'state', an integer, not an essentially Boolean type. Using non-Boolean values as conditions reduces readability and may mask logic errors where the value is non-zero but not the intended condition.
explanation.code_evidence: The provided code snippet contains an if statement with a non-Boolean controlling expression.

fix_suggestions.title: Convert if statement to a conditional expression
fix_suggestions.why: This approach converts the if statement to a conditional expression, making it clearer that a Boolean value is expected.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the if statement is only executed when the controlling expression is a Boolean value.
fix_suggestions.risk_reduction: This fix reduces the risk of logic errors and improves readability.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Convert if statement to a conditional expression

risk_analysis.why: The use of a non-Boolean value as a controlling expression can lead to logic errors and reduced readability.
potential_failures: Logic errors due to incorrect interpretation of non-Boolean values

deviation_advice.required_justification: The use of non-Boolean values as controlling expressions is not allowed according to MISRA guidelines.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all if statements use essentially Boolean types as controlling expressions.

results.guideline_title: The atof, atoi, atol and atoll functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used

explanation.summary: The code uses atoi function which is not allowed according to MISRA.
explanation.rule_basis: This code violates Rule 21.7 because the atoi function of <stdlib.h> shall not be used. Restricted standard-library features are disallowed because they reduce determinism, analyzability, or portability.
explanation.code_evidence: The atoi function is used in the code snippet.

fix_suggestions.title: Replace atoi with a safer alternative
fix_suggestions.why: The atoi function is not recommended due to its potential for undefined behavior.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix replaces the atoi function with sscanf, which is a safer alternative for converting strings to integers.
fix_suggestions.risk_reduction: Using sscanf instead of atoi reduces the risk of undefined behavior.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Replace atoi with a safer alternative

risk_analysis.why: The use of atoi function can lead to undefined behavior.
potential_failures: Undefined behavior due to incorrect input

deviation_advice.required_justification: The use of atoi function is not allowed according to MISRA.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all instances of atoi are replaced with safer alternatives.

results.guideline_title: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible

explanation.summary: The pointer parameter 'log_message' should be const-qualified.
explanation.rule_basis: This code violates Rule 8.13 because a pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided uses a non-const pointer for the 'log_message' function parameter.

fix_suggestions.title: Change the 'log_message' function parameter to const
fix_suggestions.why: Making the parameter const ensures that it points to a const-qualified type.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This change brings the code into compliance with MISRA C:2012 Rule 8.13.
fix_suggestions.risk_reduction: By making the parameter const, the risk of unintended modification of the log buffer is reduced.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Change the 'log_message' function parameter to const

risk_analysis.why: The use of a non-const pointer can lead to unintended modifications of the log buffer, which can result in incorrect logging or unexpected behavior.
potential_failures: Incorrect logging due to unintended modifications of the log buffer

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement the suggested fix to bring the code into compliance with MISRA C:2012 Rule 8.13.
deviation_advice.required_justification: The use of a non-const pointer in this context can lead to maintainability and correctness issues.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all function parameters are appropriately const-qualified.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.

explanation.summary: The code performs an implicit narrowing conversion which may lead to data loss and incorrect program behavior.
explanation.rule_basis: The value of 'total' (int) is assigned to 'small' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value (300 + total > 255), causing data loss and incorrect program behavior.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet contains an implicit narrowing conversion from int to uint8_t.

fix_suggestions.title: Explicit cast
fix_suggestions.why: To avoid implicit narrowing conversion and potential data loss.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the conversion is explicit and less prone to errors.

fix_suggestions.title: Use a larger data type
fix_suggestions.why: To prevent potential data loss due to narrowing conversion.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix reduces the risk of data loss by using a larger data type.

risk_analysis.why: The implicit narrowing conversion may lead to data loss and incorrect program behavior.
potential_failures: Data loss due to implicit narrowing conversion
Incorrect program behavior due to truncated values

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fixes.
deviation_advice.required_justification: Implicit narrowing conversion is not allowed in MISRA C.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that the fix does not introduce new issues.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear inconsistent as the warning message does not explicitly mention 'narrowing conversion'.

results.guideline_title: The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie between zero and one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand

explanation.summary: The right hand operand of a shift operator should be within the range of the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand.
explanation.rule_basis: To prevent undefined behavior and ensure safe shifts.
explanation.code_evidence: The right hand operand of the shift operator (8) is greater than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand (1U).

risk_analysis.why: The shift operator is used with a count greater than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand, which may lead to undefined behavior.
potential_failures: Data loss or incorrect program behavior due to undefined behavior.
